Package neqsim.process.safety.rupture

Class MaterialStrengthCurve

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.rupture.MaterialStrengthCurve
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class MaterialStrengthCurve extends Object implements Serializable
Temperature-dependent material strength curve for fire rupture screening.

The curve stores room-temperature yield and tensile strength together with a generic retained strength factor versus metal temperature. The default carbon-steel curve is intended for screening studies based on industry practice where detailed certified material data is not yet available. Project-specific studies should replace the curve with verified values from the applicable material certificate, pressure-piping code, or finite-element assessment.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • MaterialStrengthCurve

      public MaterialStrengthCurve(String materialName, double ambientYieldStrengthPa, double ambientTensileStrengthPa, double[] temperatureK, double[] retainedStrengthFactor, String dataSource)
      Creates a material strength curve.
      Parameters:
      materialName - material or grade name; must not be empty
      ambientYieldStrengthPa - yield strength at ambient temperature in Pa; must be positive
      ambientTensileStrengthPa - tensile strength at ambient temperature in Pa; must be positive
      temperatureK - temperature grid in K; must be strictly increasing
      retainedStrengthFactor - retained strength factors on the temperature grid; each value must be positive
      dataSource - short description of the data source or assumption basis
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the curve input is invalid

  • Method Details

    • carbonSteel

      public static MaterialStrengthCurve carbonSteel(String materialName, double yieldStrengthPa, double tensileStrengthPa)
      Creates a generic carbon-steel curve from ambient strengths.
      Parameters:
      materialName - material name
      yieldStrengthPa - ambient yield strength in Pa
      tensileStrengthPa - ambient tensile strength in Pa
      Returns:
      generic carbon-steel temperature-strength curve

    • forApi5LPipeGrade

      public static MaterialStrengthCurve forApi5LPipeGrade(String grade)
      Loads an API 5L pipe grade from the NeqSim design database.

      If the database table is unavailable, a small built-in fallback table is used for common API 5L grades. Strengths from MaterialPipeProperties are stored in psi and converted to Pa.

      Parameters:
      grade - API 5L grade, for example B, X52, X65, or X70
      Returns:
      material strength curve using the generic carbon-steel temperature reduction
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the grade is empty

      Returns:

    • retainedStrengthFactor

      public double retainedStrengthFactor(double metalTemperatureK)
      Calculates retained-strength factor by linear interpolation.
      Parameters:
      metalTemperatureK - metal temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      retained strength factor at the specified temperature
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the temperature is non-positive

    • yieldStrengthAt

      public double yieldStrengthAt(double metalTemperatureK)
      Calculates yield strength at metal temperature.

      metalTemperatureK - metal temperature in K; must be positive
      Returns:
      yield strength in Pa

      public double tensileStrengthAt(double metalTemperatureK)
      Calculates tensile strength at metal temperature.

      Returns:
      tensile strength in Pa

    • allowableRuptureStressAt

      public double allowableRuptureStressAt(double metalTemperatureK, double tensileStrengthFactor)
      Calculates allowable rupture stress at metal temperature.
      Parameters:
      metalTemperatureK - metal temperature in K; must be positive
      tensileStrengthFactor - fraction of retained tensile strength accepted for rupture screening; must be positive
      Returns:
      allowable stress in Pa

      Queries ambient API 5L strengths from the process design database.

      Returns:
      two-element array with yield and tensile strength in Pa, or null when not found
